question: Which data structure follows the Last In, First Out (LIFO) principle?
explanation: A stack follows the Last In, First Out (LIFO) principle.

question: Which data structure follows the First In, First Out (FIFO) principle?
explanation: A queue processes elements in the order they are inserted.

question: What is the index of the first element in an array in C, C++, Java and Python?
explanation: Arrays are zero-indexed in these languages.

question: Which searching algorithm requires the input array to be sorted?
explanation: Binary Search works only on sorted collections.

question: Which sorting algorithm repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order?
explanation: Bubble Sort compares and swaps adjacent elements.

question: What is the time complexity of Binary Search?
explanation: Binary Search halves the search space in every iteration.

question: What is the worst-case time complexity of Linear Search?
explanation: Linear Search may examine every element.
The array contains three elements.

question: Which operation removes the top element from a stack?
explanation: pop() removes the top element.

question: Which operation inserts an element into a queue?
explanation: enqueue() inserts an element at the rear.

question: Which data structure allows efficient insertion and deletion at arbitrary positions without shifting elements?
explanation: Linked lists adjust pointers instead of shifting elements.

question: Which tree traversal visits Root, Left, Right?
explanation: Preorder traversal follows Root → Left → Right.

question: Which tree traversal of a Binary Search Tree produces sorted output?
explanation: Inorder traversal of a BST returns elements in sorted order.

question: Every recursive function must have:
explanation: Without a base condition recursion will continue indefinitely.

question: Which sorting algorithm selects the smallest element and places it in the correct position during each pass?
explanation: Selection Sort repeatedly selects the minimum element.

question: What is the time complexity of accessing an element by index in an array?
explanation: Arrays provide constant-time random access.

question: Which data structure is commonly used to implement Breadth First Search (BFS)?
explanation: BFS explores nodes level by level using a queue.

question: Which sorting algorithm has the best average-case time complexity?
explanation: Merge Sort runs in O(n log n) in the average and worst cases.

question: Which application commonly uses a stack?
explanation: Function calls are managed using the call stack.

question: Which scheduling algorithm is naturally implemented using a queue?
explanation: Round Robin scheduling uses a queue to rotate processes.

question: Which pointer of the last node in a singly linked list contains NULL?
explanation: The next pointer of the last node is NULL.

question: What is the maximum number of children a binary tree node can have?
explanation: A binary tree node can have at most two children.

question: Which traversal technique generally uses recursion?
explanation: Depth First Search is commonly implemented recursively.

question: The primary purpose of a hash table is:
explanation: Hash tables provide fast average-case lookup, insertion and deletion.

question: Which time complexity grows the fastest as n becomes very large?
explanation: Among the given options, O(n²) grows the fastest.
a.length-1 is the index of the last element.

question: How many comparisons are needed in the worst case for Linear Search on an array of n elements?
explanation: Linear Search may examine every element.

question: Which sorting algorithm repeatedly inserts each element into its correct position in the sorted portion of the array?
explanation: Insertion Sort builds the sorted array one element at a time.

question: Ignoring constants and lower-order terms, the complexity 4n² + 3n + 2 is:
explanation: The highest-order term n² dominates.

question: Which traversal visits Left, Root, Right?
explanation: Inorder traversal follows Left → Root → Right.

question: Which data structure is used internally by Breadth First Search?
explanation: BFS processes nodes level by level using a queue.

question: Infinite recursion occurs when:
options: The recursive function has no base condition
explanation: Without a terminating condition, recursive calls never stop.

question: Which algorithm generally has an average-case time complexity of O(n log n)?
explanation: Merge Sort runs in O(n log n) average and worst case.

question: Binary Search can be applied only when the array is:
explanation: Binary Search requires the elements to be sorted.

question: What is the maximum number of comparisons required to search an element in a sorted array of 16 elements using Binary Search?
explanation: Worst-case comparisons = log₂(16) + 1 = 5.

question: In a Binary Search Tree, where is a value smaller than the root stored?
explanation: Values smaller than the root are stored in the left subtree.

question: What will be the top element after performing Push(10), Push(20), Pop(), Push(30)?
explanation: 20 is popped, then 30 is pushed. Top element is 30.

question: A queue contains: 10, 20, 30. After one dequeue and one enqueue(40), what is the front element?
explanation: 10 is removed. Queue becomes 20, 30, 40.

question: Which notation represents the upper bound of an algorithm's running time?
explanation: Big-O notation describes the upper bound (worst-case growth).

question: A hash collision occurs when:
options: Two different keys map to the same index
explanation: A collision occurs when different keys produce the same hash index.

question: Which traversal is generally preferred to find the shortest path in an unweighted graph?
explanation: Breadth First Search finds the shortest path in an unweighted graph.

question: What is the time complexity of inserting an element at the beginning of an array of size n?
explanation: All existing elements must be shifted one position.

question: Which operation is generally faster in a linked list than in an array?
explanation: Insertion at the head of a linked list is O(1).

question: Which sorting algorithm follows the Divide and Conquer technique?
explanation: Merge Sort recursively divides and merges the array.

question: Which traversal visits Left, Right, Root?
explanation: Postorder traversal follows Left → Right → Root.

question: Which complexity is considered more efficient for very large inputs?
explanation: O(n log n) grows much slower than O(n²), O(n³), or O(2ⁿ).

question: Which searching algorithm checks each element one by one until the target is found?
explanation: Linear Search examines elements sequentially.

question: Which data structure is most suitable for implementing recursion internally?
explanation: Recursive function calls are managed using the call stack.
